name: hft-quant-expert
description: Quantitative trading expertise for DeFi and crypto derivatives. Use when building trading strategies, signals, risk management. Triggers on signal, backtest, alpha, sharpe, volatility, correlation, position size, risk.
capabilities: Design entry signals using z-score calculations
Calculate position sizes with Kelly Criterion (0.25x adjustment)
Compute annualized Sharpe ratios for strategy evaluation
Identify and avoid backtesting pitfalls (lookahead bias, survivorship bias, overfitting)
Account for gas costs and slippage in DeFi trading
Calculate mean reversion half-life for pairs trading
author: BarisSozen
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. This is a documentation-only skill containing trading formulas and methodology. No executable code, network operations, or system access exists. Scanner misinterpreted trading terminology as cryptographic references and markdown code blocks as shell commands.
valueStatement: Quantitative trading in DeFi and crypto requires specialized expertise in strategy design, risk management, and backtesting. This skill provides expert guidance on z-scores, position sizing with Kelly Criterion, Sharpe ratios, and avoiding common pitfalls like lookahead bias and overfitting.
limitations: Does not execute trades or connect to exchanges
Does not provide live market data or price feeds
Does not implement specific trading algorithms in code
Does not guarantee profitable trading outcomes

useCases.title: Develop quantitative trading strategies
useCases.description: Design and refine systematic trading approaches using statistical methods like z-scores and mean reversion analysis.

useCases.title: Backtest and validate strategies
useCases.description: Test trading strategies against historical data while avoiding common errors like lookahead bias and overfitting.

useCases.title: Calculate risk and position sizes
useCases.description: Determine appropriate position sizes using Kelly Criterion and assess risk with Sharpe ratio calculations.

promptTemplates.title: Basic signal calculation
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me calculate a z-score entry signal for a trading strategy. I have price data and want to identify when to enter positions based on deviation from the rolling mean.

promptTemplates.title: Position sizing with Kelly Criterion
promptTemplates.prompt: I have a trading strategy with 55% win rate and 1.5 average win/loss ratio. Calculate the Kelly fraction and suggest a position size that accounts for the fractional Kelly approach.
promptTemplates.scenario: Determining position size with Kelly Criterion

promptTemplates.title: Backtest validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my backtesting methodology. I'm testing a mean reversion strategy on hourly data. What pitfalls should I watch for like lookahead bias, survivorship bias, and overfitting?

promptTemplates.title: Strategy performance evaluation
promptTemplates.prompt: My strategy has an annualized return of 45% with 18% volatility. Calculate the Sharpe ratio and help me evaluate if this performance is robust after accounting for trading costs and slippage.
promptTemplates.scenario: Evaluating strategy performance with Sharpe ratio

bestPractices: Use fractional Kelly (0.25x) to reduce variance and avoid ruin from inevitable losing streaks.
Always account for gas costs and slippage when backtesting DeFi strategies, as these can significantly impact net returns.
Validate backtests for lookahead bias by ensuring signals only use data available at the signal generation time.
antiPatterns: Ignoring transaction costs: Gas fees on L2s and slippage on volatile assets can turn profitable strategies into losing ones.
Overfitting to historical data: Using too many parameters or optimizing on the same data used for testing creates false confidence.
Survivorship bias: Backtesting only assets that survived eliminates failed projects from consideration, inflating returns.

faq.question: What triggers should activate this skill?
faq.answer: Use this skill when users mention signal, backtest, alpha, sharpe, volatility, correlation, position size, risk, or trading strategy.

faq.question: Does this skill execute real trades?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance and formulas only. It does not connect to exchanges or execute any trading operations.

faq.question: What is the Kelly Criterion?
faq.answer: The Kelly Criterion is a mathematical formula that calculates the optimal position size based on win probability and win/loss ratio to maximize long-term growth.

faq.question: What is z-score in trading?
faq.answer: Z-score measures how many standard deviations a current value deviates from its rolling mean. It helps identify overextended prices for mean reversion strategies.

faq.question: What is the difference between daily and hourly annualization?
faq.answer: Daily data: multiply by sqrt(252) trading days. Hourly data: multiply by sqrt(365*24) hours. Using the wrong factor produces incorrect Sharpe ratios.

faq.question: How do I avoid lookahead bias?
faq.answer: Ensure your backtest only uses data available at each point in time. Never include future information, earnings dates, or delistings that were not known when the signal was generated.
